name: Bats

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Bats (bats1242).

notes: Skeleton. Bats (Tsova-Tush) is a Nakh (Northeast Caucasian) language written in the Georgian Mkhedruli script supplemented by extra letters. The 33 standard Georgian letters take their usual phonemic values, including the ejective series; added signs write the schwa ჷ (ə), the pharyngeal ჺ (ʕ), the glottal stop ჸ (ʔ) and ჶ (f). Bats has a much larger consonant inventory than Georgian — including pharyngealized consonants, additional uvulars, a voiceless-lateral series and nasalized vowels — much of which is written with digraphs and diacritic combinations not resolved by this single-symbol map; those distinctions are left under-encoded, so a substantial PER floor is expected.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Bats (bats1242)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
